subject: TSL Weekly Strategy — Week of Mar 9, 2026
html:
TSL Internal

TSL Weekly Strategy

Week of Mar 9–13, 2026  ·  For Sam & David

What Got Done This Week

Product: Home page shipped. Homepage redesigned to single-screen (sourcing left, upload right). Pitch deck upload workflow scoped end-to-end — drag/drop → benchmarking → interested/diligence/pass. Fund evaluation framework built out with graduation rates, GP commit thresholds, valuation methodology. Two-pager identified as key differentiator over Vantager.

GTM: SF Launch Party — 13 LP/allocator attendees. Charlotte sync with deep Vantager post-mortem and direct product input. Cendana + Stifel sessions. NY event locked for April 27.

Path to First Paid Customer ($10K by May)

What needs to be true: Three things have to click in sequence. First, a working pitch deck upload demo — Charlotte needs to see it by Mar 18 and it needs to produce something she'd actually use (benchmarking output, a two-pager draft). DocSend extraction is the blocker and it hasn't moved yet. Second, a clear $10K ask with defined deliverables — right now there's no explicit pricing or offer on the table, which means even a willing buyer can't say yes. Third, the April 27 NYC event needs to convert at least one LP from "interested" to "paying" — that's 6 weeks away, which is tight.

Did this week move the needle? Partially. Product direction got sharper and the SF event built real relationships. But the demo still isn't done — which means the most important week of the next two months starts Monday with DocSend still unsolved. That's the critical path and it hasn't moved.

Most likely first customer: Charlotte. She has a KPI to automate LP workflows by June, direct access to Burgiss data, and she's already designing the product with you. The gap: she hasn't seen the demo and doesn't have platform login yet. Close both this week and she's convertible before April 27. Second candidate: someone from the SF dinner — Adrian (Equity Alliance), Max (S Cubed), or Alex (Jordan Park) are the highest-signal names from the attendee list.

Alignment Check

Aligned: Product vision is crisp — two-pager as the differentiator, community intelligence over pure data, single-screen homepage, hide everything that isn't core. That's good alignment for a two-person team.

Potential friction: There's an implicit question that hasn't been answered out loud: what is the thing you're selling right now? Is it the pitch deck upload demo, the existing platform, or the two-pager service? Sam is building toward the demo. David is running events and LP relationships. Those efforts converge at April 27 — but if there's no explicit agreement on the offer, the event generates warmth without a conversion. Worth a 15-minute conversation before the week starts.

Bandwidth & Resource Reality

Sam's plate: DocSend extraction + pitch deck demo + homepage redesign + Shy's fund database + onboarding system + Charlotte intro + Cameron email. That's too much for one week. The only thing that matters before Mar 18 is the demo. Everything else waits.

David's plate: SF follow-ups, Supabase access for Sam, Marco (VC Lab) connection, podcast logistics. The SF follow-ups are time-sensitive — momentum from the dinner fades fast. That's the highest-leverage use of David's week. Podcast B-roll and thumbnails should not be on this week's list.

What to drop this week: Podcast logistics, Supabase migration, fund database bulk upload, community events page. None of those are on the path to $10K by May. The fund database (Shy's list) is valuable but not urgent — David can start it async.

Platform Pulse

No metrics discussed this week. This is a blind spot. Recommended to track weekly: active users, funds researched, pitch decks uploaded, community notes added. Without this, you can't tell if the product is being used or just built.

Competitive Signals

  • Vantager → MSCI: Charlotte received $7K. Acqui-hire, not product acquisition. The GP integration + auditability gap is still open — that's the moat to build.
  • Velvet: Charlotte rolling off as advisor. Founder distracted, eng too slow. Not a near-term threat.
  • Erasmus: CTO/domain tension, Benchmark-backed but drifting toward pure tech. Worth watching.

Priorities for Next Week

  1. [Sam] Solve DocSend extraction and ship pitch deck upload demo — deadline Mar 18, non-negotiable
  2. [Sam] Get Charlotte platform login + Burgiss data connected before the demo
  3. [David] SF dinner follow-ups — prioritize Adrian, Max, Alex while it's fresh
  4. [Both] Align on the $10K offer before April 27 — what exactly are you selling and at what price?
  5. [David] Connect with Marco (VC Lab) re: fund pipeline

Source: Sam / David Co-Work (Mar 13), Charlotte / Sam Sync (Mar 11), TSL/Allocator Ideas Brief (Mar 9), Sam / DZ Weekly (Mar 9)
